channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: President Donald Trump has warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine or risks not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: Stephany Matat And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump FBI director nominee Kash Patel picks up support from key GOP senator
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy said Tuesday that he would vote to confirm Kash Patel as FBI director, as the Senate is expected to hold a final vote this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump moves swiftly on his agenda in a departure from his first-term stumbles
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Months into his first term as president, Donald Trump was furious with the snowballing Russia investigation and ordered White House counsel Don McGahn to make sure that special counsel Robert Mueller got fired. “Mueller has to go,” McGahn recalled Trump saying.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Trump signs executive order at Mar-a-Lago expanding IVF access
item.description: "The Order directs policy recommendations to protect IVF access and aggressively reduce out-of-pocket and health plan costs for such treatments," the White House press secretary wrote.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Zelensky 'should have never started' war with Russia
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday appeared to blame Ukraine's leaders for the three-year war with Russia, arguing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky "should have never started it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada may expand revenge porn law to cover AI
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend the state’s revenge porn law to include computer-generated images.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs executive order asking for recommendations on lowering IVF costs
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order directing federal agencies to find ways to reduce the high cost of in vitro fertilization (IVF) treatment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York's governor meets top political leaders as she weighs removing Mayor Eric Adams from office
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul held a series of meetings with key political figures Tuesday as she contemplates removing Mayor Eric Adams from his office, an unprecedented step that reflects the growing turmoil inside City Hall.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, JENNIFER PELTZ and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy to step down, asks for replacement
item.description: "I have decided it is time to start the process of identifying my successor and of preparing the Postal Service for this change,” DeJoy said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge refuses to block Musk, DOGE from 7 federal agencies
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan on Tuesday refused the request of 14 Democratic state attorneys general to immediately impose wide-ranging restrictions on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court should keep whistleblower agency head in place for now, lawyers say in reply to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court should reject an emergency appeal from the Trump administration asking for quick action to allow the firing of the head of the federal agency that protects whistleblowers, lawyers for the embattled official told the justices on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service head DeJoy to step down after 5 years marked by pandemic, losses and cost cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Louis DeJoy, the head of the U.S. Postal Service, intends to step down, the federal agency said Tuesday, after a nearly five-year tenure marked by the coronavirus pandemic, surges in mail-in election ballots and efforts to stem losses through cost and service cuts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senators ask Trump's Labor Department nominee where her allegiance would lie
item.description: Members of a Senate committee grilled Labor Secretary-designate Lori Chavez-DeRemer on Wednesday about her past support of pro-union legislation, her position on raising the federal minimum wage and her willingness to disagree with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Bussewitz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court rules Republican had no right to bring lawsuit challenging mobile voting
item.description: A divided Wisconsin Supreme Court ruled Tuesday that a Republican Party official lacked the standing to bring a lawsuit challenging the use of a mobile voting van in 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 2 Tampa Bay sheriffs to advise Florida cabinet on immigration enforcement
item.description: Two Tampa Bay area sheriffs were tapped to join the newly created State Immigration Enforcement Council on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump will sign new executive orders while his first joint TV interview with Musk airs in prime time
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday was set to sign new executive orders from his Florida home while his first joint TV interview with adviser Elon Musk airs in prime time.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge declined to block Elon Musk’s DOGE from gaining access to Education Department data on student borrowers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. and Russia discuss ending Ukraine war, without Kyiv
item.description: The talks build on last week’s phone call between President Trump and Russian leader Vladimir Putin during which the two leaders agreed to work together toward a negotiated end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes, MPR News

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: For a St. Cloud family, Trump’s pause of refugee admissions brings worry for Afghan relatives
item.description: One of President Trump’s first actions after taking office was an executive order indefinitely suspending the U.S. refugee resettlement program. A family living in St. Cloud who fled Afghanistan a year ago worries about relatives left behind.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Energy secretary blasts 'sinister' zero carbon emissions goal
item.description: “Net zero 2050 is a sinister goal, it’s a terrible goal,” Wright said to applause at the Alliance for Responsible Citizenship forum.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: NAACP guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The advisory also lists companies that have recommitted to DEI in recent weeks – such as Delta Airlines, Apple and Ben & Jerry’s.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: How President Trump is expanding the powers of the presidency
item.description: President Trump is pushing the limits of presidential power, and the courts are pushing back. MPR News host Angela Davis talks about what a president can and cannot do under the law.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: A fired national park ranger lost his dream job. He says the public is losing more
item.description: Brian Gibbs was an environmental educator at Effigy Mounds National Monument in Iowa. His job was among those nixed by the Trump administration as it pushes to shrink federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman, MPR News

item.title: CA, 18 other states' AGs celebrate injunction against Trump's citizenship order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA)—California Attorney General Rob Bonta and 18 other state attorneys general have issued a statement after another judge blocked President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to terminate birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
